title: Rotisserie Chicken, Spinach & Mushroom Gnocchi
subtitle: Rotisserie chicken folded into a deeply savory white wine cream sauce with portabellas, spinach, rosemary, thyme, and parmesan.

ingredients.heading: Ingredients
ingredients.note: Built from the chicken parmesan gnocchi base, but optimized for cooked rotisserie chicken, spinach, portabella mushrooms, and fresh herbs.
items: 4 slices thick-cut bacon, chopped
1 tbsp fresh rosemary, finely chopped
2 tbsp fresh thyme leaves, divided
2 large portabella mushroom caps, gills scraped, sliced (or 8-10 oz sliced baby bellas)
3 tbsp salted butter, divided
2 shallots, chopped
4 cloves garlic, chopped
2 tbsp all-purpose flour
1 cup dry white wine
1 cup chicken broth
3/4 cup heavy cream
1/4 tsp nutmeg
1/2 tsp paprika
Pinch of cayenne
1 lb potato gnocchi (shelf-stable or refrigerated, not boiled)
1 rotisserie chicken, meat pulled and shredded (about 3-4 cups)
4 packed cups baby spinach
3/4 cup grated parmesan, divided, plus more for serving
1/2 lemon, juiced (optional but excellent)
Kosher salt and black pepper
ingredients.callout: Maximum flavor move: Brown the mushrooms hard before the sauce. Portabellas carry a lot of moisture, so let them give it up, then keep cooking until the edges get deeply browned.

shoppingList.item: Baby spinach (1 large clamshell or bag)

body.html: A high-flavor, one-skillet gnocchi bake: bacon crisps with rosemary, portabellas brown in the drippings, shallots and garlic build the base, then wine, broth, cream, parmesan, rotisserie chicken, spinach, and raw gnocchi turn into a bubbling skillet dinner.

items: Crisp bacon with rosemary, then save it for the finish
Brown portabellas hard in the bacon fat
Bloom shallots, garlic, thyme, paprika, cayenne, and nutmeg
Reduce wine, then whisk in broth, cream, and parmesan
Cook gnocchi in the sauce, then fold in chicken and spinach
Bake until bubbling, finish with bacon, herbs, lemon, and parmesan

body.html: Inspired by Chew Out Loud's one-pot chicken gnocchi: raw gnocchi cooks directly in the sauce, spinach goes in at the end, and a little nutmeg gives the cream sauce that cozy restaurant note.

slides.title: Crisp Bacon + Bloom Rosemary
slides.checkboxLabel: Bacon crisped with rosemary, fat left in pan

items: Preheat oven to 425°F with a rack in the upper-middle position.
In a deep oven-safe skillet, cook bacon over medium heat until crisp, about 5-7 minutes.
During the last minute, stir in the rosemary so it blooms and crisps in the bacon fat.
Transfer bacon and rosemary to a plate. Leave the fat in the skillet.

body.html: Flavor base: This gives you smoky fat plus fried rosemary before anything else hits the pan.

items: Raise heat to medium-high. Add mushrooms in a single-ish layer with a pinch of salt.
Cook without fussing until they release liquid, the liquid cooks off, and the edges brown, 6-8 minutes.
Add 1 tbsp butter and 1 tbsp thyme. Toss 30 seconds until glossy and fragrant.
Transfer mushrooms to the bacon plate.

body.html: Do not rush this: Pale mushrooms taste watery. Brown mushrooms taste meaty.

slides.title: Build the Sauce
slides.checkboxLabel: Aromatics cooked, wine reduced, cream sauce thickened

items: Lower heat to medium. Add 2 tbsp butter, then shallots, garlic, remaining thyme, paprika, cayenne, nutmeg, salt, and black pepper.
Cook 1-2 minutes, scraping up the browned bits and letting the aromatics soften.
Sprinkle in 2 tbsp flour and stir constantly for 1 minute so it loses the raw taste.
Pour in wine, scraping hard. Simmer until reduced by about half, 3-5 minutes.
Whisk in chicken broth, then heavy cream. Simmer 2-3 minutes until lightly thickened.
Stir in 1/4 cup parmesan until melted.

body.html: Texture target: Sauce should coat a spoon but still look loose. The gnocchi and oven finish will tighten it.

slides.title: Gnocchi, Chicken + Spinach
slides.checkboxLabel: Gnocchi tender, chicken warmed, spinach wilted

items: Stir raw gnocchi into the sauce and simmer gently 3-5 minutes, until the pieces begin to float and soften.
Fold in rotisserie chicken and the browned mushrooms.
Add spinach by handfuls, folding just until it starts to wilt.
Taste the sauce. Add salt, black pepper, and a squeeze of lemon if it needs brightness.

body.html: Rotisserie rule: Add the chicken late. It is already cooked, so the goal is juicy and warm, not simmered to shreds.

slides.title: Parmesan Bake + Serve
slides.checkboxLabel: Baked until bubbling, topped with bacon and herbs

items: Sprinkle remaining 1/2 cup parmesan evenly over the skillet.
Bake uncovered at 425°F for 10-12 minutes, until bubbling at the edges and the gnocchi is tender.
Optional: broil 1-2 minutes for a browned top.
Rest 5 minutes, then top with the crisp bacon-rosemary mixture, extra parmesan, black pepper, and a few fresh thyme leaves.

body.html: Serve it hot: You want creamy sauce clinging to gnocchi, browned mushrooms tucked throughout, spinach just wilted, and crunchy bacon on top.
